title: Coffee with Cream on Carpet
stainChemistry: Coffee with cream is a three-component stain: (1) COFFEE TANNIN, (2) MILK PROTEIN, and (3) MILK FAT (butterfat from the cream). Ordering is critical because the three families respond to different and sometimes OPPOSITE chemistries: hot water and alkaline cleaners (OxiClean) set the protein permanently; alkaline cleaners also DARKEN the tannin permanently. Heat anywhere before protein is dealt with = locked in. The correct order is: cold water + Dawn (universal-safe, handles fresh tannin + lifts fat) → enzyme pretreat (warm, not hot — digests protein) → 3% peroxide (oxidizes residual tannin) → vinegar rinse. On carpet the additional constraint is PAD WICKING: over-wetting carries the mess into the pad where you can't reach it. Damp cloth, not pouring.
whyThisWorks: Cold + Dawn deals with fresh tannin + fat in one step without setting protein. Enzyme in warm (not hot) water digests the milk protein. Peroxide then reaches residual tannin. Vinegar keeps tannin on the light (acid) side. Weighted paper towels overnight prevent pad wick-back. Order + cold + no-pouring = clean. Wrong order = ghost.
defaultAssumption: Morning spill on living-room / home-office carpet. Fresh = reasonable win. Overnight + carpet-pad-soaked = harder; tends to leave a tan halo that returns next day as moisture wicks back. Barista-style cappuccino (more milk) = bigger protein + fat component. Black coffee with a splash = closer to plain coffee chemistry.

spottingProtocol.equipment: White paper towels or cotton cloth
spottingProtocol.instruction: Press paper towels straight down on the spill. Lift, rotate, press again. Outside-in. No rubbing. Keep blotting until the towel stops picking up color.

spottingProtocol.technique: Flush fresh tannin without pad saturation
spottingProtocol.equipment: Cold water, white cloth, dry towel + weight
spottingProtocol.instruction: Dampen a cloth with cold water, press. Lift, blot with dry cloth, repeat 2–3 passes. COLD only — warm/hot water sets the milk milk protein permanently. Never pour — pad soak = permanent ghost.

spottingProtocol.agent: Dawn + Cold Water on Damp Cloth
spottingProtocol.technique: Universal-safe surfactant — lifts fat + fresh tannin
spottingProtocol.equipment: Dawn + cold water on damp cloth, soft brush
spottingProtocol.instruction: Dawn in cold water on damp cloth. Work in with soft toothbrush. Dwell 5 min. Blot. Dawn lifts fat + fresh tannin without setting protein. NO OxiClean — alkaline sets protein AND darkens tannin.

spottingProtocol.equipment: OxiClean Versatile / Biz in WARM water (<100°F / 38°C) on damp cloth
spottingProtocol.instruction: Dissolve enzyme pretreat in WARM water — bath temperature, NOT hot. Damp cloth, press on spot, cover with damp towel to keep enzyme wet. 15–20 min. Hot water = protein set.
Hidden-area test first on dyed carpet. 3% peroxide damp cloth 5–10 min for residual brown. Order matters — peroxide BEFORE enzyme sets protein. Don't mix with ammonia / bleach / alkaline.

spottingProtocol.technique: Neutralize + keep tannin on acid side
spottingProtocol.equipment: 1 tbsp vinegar + 1 cup cool water on damp cloth
spottingProtocol.instruction: Vinegar rinse on damp cloth. Neutralizes peroxide + keeps tannin light. Blot dry.
Weighted paper towels overnight. Fan-dry. Pulls residual moisture + tannin out of pad before wick-back. Inspect in daylight — halo = one more peroxide + vinegar round.

steps: 2. Cold damp-cloth flush — no pouring, avoid pad soak.
3. Dawn + cold water damp cloth + soft brush.
4. Enzyme pretreat WARM (not hot), 15–20 min — BEFORE oxidizer.
5. 3% H₂O₂ damp cloth for residual tannin — AFTER enzyme.
professionalProtocol.temperature: cold through initial; warm (<100°F) for enzyme; never hot
warnings: NEVER hot water — sets milk protein
NEVER oxidizer BEFORE enzyme — sets protein permanently
NEVER alkaline cleaners (OxiClean alone, ammonia, baking soda) — sets protein AND darkens tannin
Never pour — pad wick-back is the ceiling

homeSolutions.agent: Three Stains in One — Order Matters
homeSolutions.instruction: Coffee with cream is actually three stains at once: coffee tannin (brown pigment), milk protein, and milk fat. These respond to DIFFERENT chemistries, and some are OPPOSITES — alkaline cleaners (OxiClean, ammonia) set the milk protein permanently AND darken the tannin permanently. Hot water cooks the milk protein into the carpet. The order is: cold water + Dawn first, then a WARM (not hot) enzyme soak to digest the protein, then peroxide for any remaining brown, then a vinegar rinse. Wrong order = permanent tan halo.

Press a thick stack of white paper towels or a clean cloth STRAIGHT DOWN on the spill. Lift, move to a dry section, press again. Work from the outside of the spill inward to contain it. Do NOT rub — rubbing pushes coffee + cream into the pad and pad contamination is where rings come back from.

Pour a SMALL amount of cold water on the spot and blot with a dry cloth. Repeat 2–3 times. Cold ONLY — warm/hot water sets the milk protein permanently, even if the coffee was hot. DO NOT flood — water that reaches the pad carries the stain deeper where you can't extract it.

Mix 1 tablespoon of Dawn in 2 cups cold water. Dampen a white cloth in the solution and press on the spot with a soft toothbrush for 5 minutes. Blot with a clean damp cloth. Dawn is safe on everything — it lifts fresh tannin and fat without setting protein. DO NOT use OxiClean or baking soda at this stage — alkaline cleaners set protein AND darken tannin.

homeSolutions.agent: Enzyme Pretreat in WARM (Not Hot) Water
homeSolutions.instruction: Dissolve OxiClean Versatile or Biz in WARM water — bath temperature, under 100°F. NOT hot. Warm is necessary for enzyme activity; hot would set the protein you're trying to remove. Dampen a white cloth in the enzyme solution, press on the spot, cover with a second damp towel to keep it wet, wait 15–20 minutes. The enzyme digests the milk protein from the cream.

Once protein is digested, 3% drugstore peroxide on a damp cloth for 5–10 minutes oxidizes any residual tannin. Hidden-area test first on dyed carpet. Only AFTER the enzyme step — peroxide BEFORE enzyme sets the protein.
homeSolutions.safetyNote: Never mix peroxide with ammonia, bleach, OxiClean, or baking soda.

homeSolutions.instruction: Mix 1 tablespoon white vinegar + 1 cup cool water. Dab on damp cloth, press 5 min. Neutralizes peroxide + keeps residual tannin in its lighter (acid) color. Blot dry.

Stack 6–10 dry paper towels on the spot, put a heavy book on top, leave overnight. Fan-dry in the morning. Pulls residual moisture + coffee pigment out of the pad before it wicks back up as a visible ring next day. Inspect in daylight. Halo remaining = one more peroxide + vinegar round.

homeSolutions.agent: Escalate for Pad Saturation or Ring Return
homeSolutions.instruction: Call a pro if the stain dried overnight + saturated the pad, if a tan halo returns after a full home cycle, or if someone hit the spot with OxiClean / baking soda before you got there. Tell them: "Coffee with cream on [nylon / polyester / wool] carpet, age [fresh / overnight], home-treated in order: cold + Dawn → enzyme (warm, not hot) → 3% peroxide → vinegar → overnight paper towels. No alkaline pre-treatment used [or: alkaline was used before I got here]."

steps: 3. Cold water small-volume, blot between, 2–3 passes.
4. Dawn + cold water damp cloth + soft brush, 5 min.
5. Enzyme pretreat WARM (not hot) water, 15–20 min.
6. 3% peroxide damp cloth AFTER enzyme, 5–10 min.
diyProtocol.whenToStop: Stop if pad is saturated, if OxiClean / baking soda was used before you (tannin likely locked), if ring returns after two rounds, or if hot water was applied before the cold step.
warnings: NEVER hot water — sets milk protein permanently
NEVER oxidizer before enzyme — sets protein
NEVER alkaline cleaners on the tannin — darkens brown permanently

neverDo: Never hot water or steam on cream-containing spills
Never OxiClean / baking soda / ammonia before enzyme
Never rub — drives stain into pad
fiberSensitivities: Nylon / polyester / olefin carpet: tolerates full protocol
Wool: pH-sensitive — pro lane for tight spots
Dyed carpet: hidden peroxide test mandatory

materialWarnings: Three stains in one — order matters
Alkaline cleaners set tannin AND protein — double lose
Cold water throughout the wet phase

escalation.when: Pad saturation, stain dried overnight with heat exposure, ring returns after two home rounds, or alkaline cleaner (OxiClean / baking soda) was applied before the enzyme step.
escalation.whatToTell: "Coffee with cream on [nylon / polyester / wool] carpet, age [fresh / overnight], home-treated in order: cold + Dawn → enzyme (warm, not hot) → 3% peroxide → vinegar → overnight paper towels. No hot water / no oxidizer before enzyme. [Residual halo / pad saturation / alkaline was used before I got here]."
escalation.specialistType: professional carpet cleaner with combination-stain chemistry

contextQuestions: Cappuccino / latte (more milk) or splash of cream?
Has anyone hit the spot with OxiClean / baking soda already?
deepSolveHooks.modifierNotes: Flavored-syrup drinks add sugar (sticky matrix, more cycles). Cappuccino has more protein + fat than a standard coffee-with-cream splash. The OxiClean-first mistake is the common failure path — tannin darkens AND protein sets, both locked.
